skillName: Order any integers, negatives and decimals
misconceptionSummary: Students think zero is either the smallest number or not part of the ordering at all.

visuals.ruleText: Every number — negative, zero, decimal, integer — has one fixed position on the same number line
visuals.subText: Zero is the dividing point: negatives left, positives right

steps.narration: There is only one number line and every number lives on it. Zero sits at the centre. All negatives are to its left; all positives, including decimals, are to its right.

lines.text: Zero is greater than every negative number
Zero is less than every positive number

steps.narration: Zero is not the smallest number — every negative is smaller. And zero is not the largest — every positive is larger. Zero is exactly between the two worlds.

lines.text: Zero sits in the middle if present
Within each group: further from zero = more extreme

steps.narration: Split any mixed set into three groups: negatives, zero, positives. Negatives go first in ascending order — most negative first. Then zero. Then positives in ascending order.
Negative three, negative one point two, zero, zero point seven, two. The structure of the number line makes the order clear.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: treating zero as the smallest or ignoring it in the ordering. Zero is greater than all negatives and less than all positives — it belongs between them.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Zero is not the smallest number — every negative is smaller than zero. Place zero between the negatives and the positives where it belongs.
